sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Oud-Heverlee Leuven are showing signs of improvement, with an unbeaten run in their last four matches, including a strong 4-0 win over KAA Gent. Sint-Truidense VV, on the other hand, are in excellent form, winning three of their last five matches, including back-to-back clean sheet victories. Their defensive solidity is evident with no goals conceded in the last three games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head encounters, matches between these teams have been tight, with both games producing goals from both sides. Oud-Heverlee Leuven won the most recent clash 3-2 at home, while Sint-Truidense VV secured a 2-1 victory in their earlier meeting this season.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Sint-Truidense VV sit higher in the league table (5th with 24 points) compared to Oud-Heverlee Leuven (13th with 15 points), reflecting their better consistency and attacking output with 19 goals scored versus Leuven’s 15. Leuven struggle with ball possession (44.3%, 15th in the league) and have one of the highest foul counts, often leading to cards (3.07 yellows per game).

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Sint-Truidense VV, Ryotaro Ito has been exceptional, with a high Sofascore rating (7.47) and key contributions in goals and assists. Leuven will rely on Ewoud Pletinckx, who has outperformed his xG with 3 goals from defense, though missing key midfielders like Siebe Schrijvers could hurt their creativity.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Sint-Truidense VV’s current form and superior league standing, along with Sofascore’s prediction favoring an away win (47%), they appear to have the edge. However, Leuven’s recent home form and resilience in head-to-head matches suggest they could make it competitive, likely resulting in a close game with goals on both sides.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Sint-Truidense VV holds a narrow lead over Oud-Heverlee Leuven with the scoreline at 1-2. The first half was action-packed with three goals, showcasing both teams' attacking intent. Sint-Truidense took the lead early through a penalty, while Leuven responded late in the half to reduce the deficit. Both sides have had their moments, but Sint-Truidense's clinical finishing has given them the edge so far.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Sint-Truidense VV has shown greater efficiency in front of goal despite having fewer shots on target than Leuven. Their ability to create and convert big chances has been the difference, capitalizing on key opportunities. On the other hand, Oud-Heverlee Leuven has shown resilience, managing to get a goal back just before the break. Leuven's dominance in duels and aerial battles suggests they can compete physically, but they’ll need to be sharper in the final third to turn the game around.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum review
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Sint-Truidense started with sustained attacking spells, particularly in the early stages, building pressure that led to their opening goal. Leuven struggled to respond initially but gained traction as the half progressed, with notable attacking surges in the middle and towards the end of the half, eventually resulting in their goal. Sint-Truidense, however, managed to regain some control in the closing minutes, ensuring they maintained their lead at the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Oud-Heverlee Leuven, Thibaud Verlinden has been a standout, already finding the net and posing a constant threat. His ability to create chances could be crucial in the second half. On the Sint-Truidense side, Ryotaro Ito’s composure from the penalty spot and involvement in creating opportunities make him a danger man. Ilias Sebaoui also made an impact with a well-taken goal, and his confidence could lead to more contributions.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: Oud-Heverlee Leuven is likely to push forward aggressively in search of an equalizer, leveraging their strength in duels to win back possession in advanced areas. They might focus on utilizing Verlinden’s creativity to unlock Sint-Truidense’s defense. Sint-Truidense, on the other hand, may adopt a more balanced approach, looking to protect their lead while exploiting counter-attacking opportunities. Their efficiency in converting chances could see them extend their advantage if Leuven overcommits.
